text: The video shows a significant endometriosis lesion on the bowel, leading to a visible stricture in the sigmoid colon. The surgical team performs a bowel resection due to the depth of the lesion.

The surgeon performs an end-to-end anastomosis of the sigmoid colon using a stapler. The stapler is inserted through the rectum, and the two bowel segments are approximated and stapled circumferentially, creating a sealed connection. Fluorescence imaging is used to confirm good vascularization of the re-approximated segments.

The surgeon addresses endometriosis on the pelvic sidewall, removing lesions from the underside of the left ovary and freeing the right ovary from adhesion to the pelvic sidewall. Fluorescence is used to identify the ureter, aiding in the procedure.

The surgeon identifies and excises a small endometrioma on the underside of the ovary. The excised sample is placed in a bag and extracted through a small incision. The ureter is highlighted in green, having been flushed with icy green dye via cystoscopy and catheterization.
